section_title: Height and setback - pitched-roof building envelope (R1 through R5)

source.document_currency_banner_note: City of Yes for Housing Opportunity moved the R1-R5 residential bulk (height and setback) regulations into the ZR 23-42 series with a Last Amended date of 12/5/2024. The site-wide currency banner was not captured for this retrieval and is a needs_review item.
source.verification_required: Draft capture of ZR 23-421 supplied by official-source-researcher (access 2026-07-22, zr.planning.nyc.gov). It has NOT been re-verified byte-for-byte against raw HTML by the rules-engineer, so raw_html_verified is false and extraction_status is extracted_draft. The governing section, the exact heights/depths, the district-to-statement association, and every applicability condition (building type, street width, qualifying-residential-site geography, special-district/overlay/historic-district context) MUST be confirmed by a qualified zoning professional (G6) before any rule citing this snapshot is published or Verified.

verbatim_excerpt: Perimeter walls are subject to setback regulations at a maximum height above the base plane of 25 feet. These planes start at the maximum permitted height of the perimeter walls and meet at a ridge line of 35 feet above the base plane.
notes: Governs the R5A pitched-roof envelope: perimeter-wall height max 25 ft above the base plane; ridge/building height max 35 ft above the base plane. Applicability is conditional on building type (detached / semi-detached / zero-lot-line per §23-421); the setback is governed by pitched-plane (sky-exposure) geometry, NOT the flat §23-423 10/15-ft rule.
section_title is a descriptive label; the official heading is pending raw-HTML verification.
Captured for M4-T006 as a governing-source candidate for the R5-series height & setback DRAFT rule family (needs_review).
Nothing in this record is a Verified zoning determination. Verified requires an M4 published rule plus G6 qualified-human approval (owner directive item 5).
